channel.title: Jsondecodeerror on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Jsondecodeerror on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] Python json.JSONDecodeError — Invalid JSON
item.description:
json.JSONDecodeError — Invalid JSON

A json.JSONDecodeError is raised when json.loads() or json.load() receives a string that is not valid JSON. This includes syntax errors like trailing commas, single quotes, unquoted keys, or malformed data.

Description

JSON has strict syntax rules. Unlike Python dictionaries, JSON requires double quotes for strings, does not allow trailing commas, and does not support comments. json.JSONDecodeError inherits from ValueError and includes the line number and position of the error.

item.title: [Solution] Python JSONDecodeError: Expecting Value — Invalid JSON Fix
item.description:
JSONDecodeError: Expecting Value

A json.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0) is raised when json.loads() receives a string that does not contain valid JSON. The most common cause is receiving an empty response, HTML error page, or non-JSON data when expecting JSON.

Description

JSON has strict syntax requirements. json.JSONDecodeError inherits from ValueError and includes the line number and character position where parsing failed. The error message Expecting value specifically means the parser expected a JSON value (string, number, object, array, true, false, or null) but found something else.
